articles.title: Prior Year Bills
articles.summary: Transfers a small balance from the FY26 HR Pre-employment Physicals account to pay an outstanding prior-year bill of the Town.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $140 from the Fiscal Year 2026 Human Resources Pre-employment Physicals Account for the purpose of paying prior year, unpaid bills of the Town, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Free Cash Transfer: Snow and Ice Deficit
articles.summary: Covers the FY26 Snow & Ice budget shortfall using Certified Free Cash — a routine action when a harsh winter exceeds the original appropriation.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $285,000 from Certified Free Cash for the purpose of funding the Snow & Ice Fiscal Year 2026 appropriation deficit, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Free Cash Transfer: Assessors' Valuation Account
articles.summary: Transfers Free Cash to cover a funding gap in the Assessors' Annual Valuation Account used for property tax assessments.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $14,600 from Certified Free Cash for the purpose of funding the Assessors' Annual Valuation Account, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Fund Record Digitization Project
articles.summary: Appropriates funds to digitize Town records for long-term preservation and public access, funded by Free Cash ($100K), Water Enterprise retained earnings ($50K), and Sewer Enterprise retained earnings ($50K).
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to appropriate $200,000 for the purpose of preserving Town records through digitization, $100,000 to be funded from Certified Free Cash, $50,000 from Water Enterprise Retained Earnings, and $50,000 from Sewer Enterprise Retained Earnings, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Sewer Enterprise Retained Earnings Transfer: Replace Vacuum Excavator
articles.summary: Transfers Sewer Enterprise retained earnings to replace an aging vacuum excavator used for sewer line maintenance and repair work.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer from Sewer Enterprise Fund retained earnings the sum of $35,000 for the purpose of funding the replacement of a vacuum excavator and for the payment of all other incidental and related costs, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Transfer 120 Main St Site Cleanup Balance to Capital Stabilization
articles.summary: Redirects $90,000 in leftover funds from a completed environmental site cleanup at 120 Main Street into the Town's new Capital Stabilization Fund.
articles.text:
To see if the Town will vote to transfer the sum of $90,000 from the funds authorized under the prior Town Meeting Article as shown in Table 1 below, which amount is no longer needed for such purposes, to the Capital Stabilization Fund as shown in Table 2 below, or act in any manner relating thereto.

TRANSFER FROM:
  11/12/24 · Article 3 · 120 Main St Environmental/Site Cleanup · $90,000
  Total: $90,000

TRANSFER TO:
  Capital Stabilization Fund · $90,000
  Total: $90,000

articles.title: Repurpose Funds for Memorial School Roof to McGovern Bathroom Project, High School Air Quality Mitigation, and Balance to Capital Stabilization
articles.summary: Redirects unspent Memorial School Roof Replacement funds to: McGovern School bathroom upgrades ($223K), High School air quality mitigation ($40K), and Capital Stabilization ($92.4K).
articles.text:
To see if the Town will vote to transfer the sum of $355,425.70 from the funds authorized under the prior Town Meeting Article as shown in Table 1 below, which amount is no longer needed for such purposes, to the projects and in the amounts listed in Table 2 below, or act in any manner relating thereto.

TRANSFER FROM:
  5/13/24 · Article 12 · Memorial School Roof Replacement · $355,425.70
  Total: $355,425.70

TRANSFER TO:
  5/12/25 Annual Art. 14 · McGovern School Bathroom Upgrades · $223,000.00
  New · High School Air Quality Mitigation · $40,000.00
  New · Capital Stabilization Fund · $92,425.70
  Total: $355,425.70

articles.title: Repurpose Monetary Articles: School Projects
articles.summary: Consolidates leftover balances from five completed school projects (track repair, cafeteria water heater, HVAC upgrades, sidewalks, facilities vehicle) into the High School Boiler replacement project.
articles.text:
To see if the Town will vote to transfer the sum of $154,780 from the funds authorized under the prior Town Meeting Articles as shown in Table 1 below, which amounts are no longer needed for such purposes, to the project and in the amount listed in Table 2 below, or act in any manner relating thereto.

TRANSFER FROM:
  6/20/20 Annual Art. 7 · High School Track Repair · $32,660.29
  5/8/23 Annual Art. 7 · High School Cafeteria Water Heater · $12,912.76
  5/12/25 Special Art. 5 · HVAC Upgrades · $67,779.20
  5/12/25 Annual Art. 10 · School Sidewalks · $41,265.00
  5/12/25 Annual Art. 10 · School Facilities Vehicle · $162.75
  Total: $154,780.00

TRANSFER TO:
  5/12/25 Annual Art. 15 · High School Boiler · $154,780.00
